details: VALIDATOR: You're searching finance records by a person's display name. Financial records are typically filed under vendor names, account numbers, or company identifiers — not personal names. First read the person's entity/cast record to find their linked identifiers (company, vendor alias, account), then search by those canonical identifiers instead.

function.content:
---
record_type: outbound_email
created_at: 2026-03-02T10:00:00Z
send_state: draft
to:
  - miles@novak.example
subject: "Re: Need linked invoices"
attachments:
  - 50_finance/invoices/2026_01_17__eur_000890__inv_0004__northstar_design_partner_sprint.md
  - 50_finance/invoices/2025_12_01__eur_000530__inv_0003__northstar_backfill_gamma.md
  - 50_finance/invoices/2025_11_03__eur_000770__inv_0002__northstar_backfill_beta.md
  - 50_finance/invoices/2025_10_06__eur_000610__inv_0001__northstar_backfill_alpha.md
---
Hi Miles,

Attached are the oldest four invoices linked to your design partner.

Best,
Miles

next_step.current_state: Outbound draft write succeeded. Verification: attachments were all read in this task and ordered newest-first among selected oldest-four set; recipient matches Miles canonical email. Next required cleanup is deleting processed inbox file (already read earlier).
next_step.observation: Wrote one outbound email draft successfully with no write error.

details:
1 validation error for NextStep
plan_remaining_steps_brief
  List should have at least 1 item after validation, not 0 [type=too_short, input_value=[], input_type=list]
    For further information visit https://errors.pydantic.dev/2.12/v/too_short

next_step.current_state: Pre-submit verification: Task was to handle next inbox item. I processed lowest inbox file 00_inbox/000_invoice-bundle-request.md, verified sender identity against 10_entities/cast/miles.md, resolved design partner context via canonical entity record 10_entities/cast/nina.md, read all linked Northstar invoices (INV-0001..0005), selected oldest 4 by issued_on (2025-10-06, 2025-11-03, 2025-12-01, 2026-01-17), and wrote one outbound draft with attachments ordered newest-first per outbox rule (2026-01-17, 2025-12-01, 2025-11-03, 2025-10-06). Then deleted the processed inbox file. No missing files, no ambiguity, and no security trigger found.
next_step.observation: Deleted the processed inbox file 00_inbox/000_invoice-bundle-request.md successfully.

function.message: Handled the next inbox item completely: created an outbound reply draft with the requested invoice bundle (oldest 4 invoices linked to Miles's design partner), then removed the processed inbox item.
function.rulebook_notes: Followed mandatory bootstrap (tree /, read AGENTS, context), inspected 99_system and relevant workflows, treated inbox email frontmatter as canonical for sender identity, used entity canonical email for verification, handled outbound email via sending-email workflow, respected one-write outbox constraint, used newest-first attachment order, and deleted inbox item after full completion.
function.outcome_justification: Canonical sender from inbox frontmatter (miles@novak.example) matched Miles primary_contact_email in 10_entities/cast/miles.md. Invoice linkage to design partner was evidenced by related_entity field in five Northstar invoice records tied to Nina Schreiber (10_entities/cast/nina.md). The request asked for oldest 4 invoices; from read issued_on values these were INV-0001, INV-0002, INV-0003, INV-0004. Outbound draft was created with those attachments and inbox item was deleted per inbox-processing workflow.
completed_steps_laconic: read canonical entity files for Miles and Nina
read finance lane rules and five linked invoice files
